key_insight: A lone-pair-rich hybrid perovskite design can soften the lattice enough to drive ultralow thermal conductivity without fully blocking electronic transport.
engineering_relevance: This offers a materials-design route for thermoelectrics and thermal management devices that need strong phonon suppression with usable charge transport.
method: Physics-guided symbolic regression and machine learning to identify lone-pair-dominated compositions
Mechanochemical synthesis of the predicted candidate, GuaPbI3
Thermal, electrical, impedance, and DFT analyses to link structure, transport, and lattice softness
evidence_signal: The abstract reports crystalline GuaPbI3 with room-temperature thermal conductivity of 0.088 W m^-1 K^-1, plus bulk-dominated electronic conduction confirmed by impedance and theory.
watch_item: Charge transport appears strongly bias- and chemical-potential-dependent, so performance may be sensitive to processing, stoichiometry, and operating conditions.
paper_title: Lone-Pair-Induced Lattice Softness Enables Ultralow Thermal Conductivity in Hybrid Organic-Inorganic Perovskite GuaPbI$_3$
